business_ideas.idea: Religion as business model: eternal LTV, volunteer labor, franchisees, tax-exempt status
Team Jorge: Israeli psyops as service hacking phones, creating fake narratives, election meddling
Parallel Live: fake live stream app simulating 50K viewers for social media clout
Ministry Brands: PE roll-up acquiring church software businesses for SaaS arbitrage
SEO content farms: Red Ventures model ranking articles then promoting affiliate links

predictions.text: Team Jorge pricing is too low, should charge 3x more for the value provided
Fake live stream apps will become ubiquitous as people chase clout online

dynamics: Sam and Shaan interview Ben Wilson, a Mormon who provides insider perspective on LDS church finances. Sam drives with detailed research and storytelling. Shaan adds skeptical commentary. Significant portion devoted to James Curry meeting with Shaan describing partnership frameworks and network effects in depth.
frameworks: Religion business model: tax exemption + volunteer labor + high LTV (eternal) + network effects
Status ladder positioning: McDonald's mass-market vs. Michelin niche positioning for content
The sleep test: resolve partnership disagreements by asking who cares most emotionally
Giving contest: frame partnerships as competition to give more, not receive more
Network density matters more than individual talent at core vs. periphery
Stay in center of network: physical proximity and institutional gravity create 10x outcomes
learnings: Religion generates $7-30B annually per major church through tithing with minimal customer acquisition cost
Hedge funds tied to religious institutions can accumulate $100B+ while claiming transparency concerns
Psychological warfare can be commercialized at scale using fake social media profiles and narrative engineering
SEO content farms rank by gaming algorithms, then promote affiliate products regardless of editorial merit
Long-term partnerships succeed when both parties frame decisions through 20-year horizon, not quarterly wins
Being at network periphery (even as successful individual) generates 10x less value than being at core

banger_quotes.quote: Religion is the greatest business of all time. Eternal LTV.
It's a rainy day fund. It just happens to be for a household of 200 million people.
You should stay in the middle. Don't slide down-market for views.
Make it a giving contest, not about what you get.

banger_quotes.who: Guest (Mark Zuckerberg via Stan via Sam)
banger_quotes.quote: You're smart enough to know when somebody else got lucky too.

numbers_dropped: Mormon church hedge fund: $100B+ in assets, $40B in US stocks alone
Mormon church leader salaries: $170K/year plus Toyota Highlander
Mormon church donations to charity since 1985: $64M
Ministry Brands: 30 church software companies acquired, $100M EBITDA
Team Jorge: $50K to hack phone, $400K retainer, $6M for election meddling
Team Jorge fake profiles: 39,000 fake social media accounts deployed
notable: Deep exploration of religion as exemplary business model followed by psyops-as-service company; significant focus on James Curry's networking philosophy and partnership longevity; frames SEO/content farms as industrialized misinformation.
